title: Seer Forecast Object
description: The complete forecast lifecycle object. Contains the three locked phases (specification, evidence, forecast), optional monitoring notes, and resolution data when scored.

id.description: Unique forecast identifier. Format: SEER-YYYY-NNNN. E.g., SEER-2026-0001.

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of forecast object creation.

status.description: Current lifecycle state. Draft: in progress. Locked: all three locks confirmed. Live: published and monitoring. Resolved: horizon reached. Scored: accuracy computed. Archived: no longer active. Moot: invalidated by exogenous event.

specification.description: Lock A: the forecastable object definition.

specification_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the specification JSON at lock time.

evidence_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the evidence summary JSON at lock time.

forecast_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the forecast JSON at lock time.

landing_confidence.description: Current confidence estimate, updated at check-ins. Mutable — not part of any lock.

monitoring.description: Optional mutable monitoring layer. Lightweight notation in v0.

brier_score.description: Brier score for the primary prediction. Lower is better. 0 = perfect, 0.25 = coin flip.

log_score.description: Logarithmic scoring rule. More sensitive to confident wrong predictions.

differential_accuracy.description: Seer score minus baseline score. Positive = Seer outperformed. This is the metric that earns Genesis its place.

baseline_outcome.description: What the declared baseline actually predicted and how it scored.

calibration_notes.description: Analyst notes on what the scoring reveals about model calibration.

resolution.description: Populated when the forecast resolves and is scored.

prediction_date.description: The declared temporal cutoff. Research was restricted to evidence available on or before this date.

time_boxed.description: Confirmation that temporal cutoff was enforced.

retrodiction.description: Present only for retrodiction runs. Records the temporal cutoff and scoring against known outcomes.

predecessor.description: ID of the forecast this one supersedes, if any.

successor.description: ID of the forecast that supersedes this one, if any.

family.description: Prediction family ID if this forecast shares a research pass with others.
